assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response explains Rapid gets new versions within ~2 weeks vs Stable at 6-8 weeks, states 'Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability' while Stable has 'Full SLA coverage', and mentions patches vs minor version behavior when cluster is ahead of channel

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response clearly states in the version warning section that after migration the cluster will remain at 1.32 and 'will NOT receive auto-upgrades until Stable catches up to 1.32', indicating no immediate version change

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response warns about 1.32 (current version) not receiving upgrades until Stable catches up, but specifically says 'patches only, no minor upgrades' which contradicts the expectation that patches would also be blocked

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update YOUR_CLUSTER --zone YOUR_ZONE --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Response includes the specific command 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone YOUR_ZONE --format="yaml(channels.stable)"' and emphasizes checking version availability in the Pre-Migration Assessment section

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Response explains the change from Rapid's ~2 weeks to Stable's 6-8 weeks timeline and discusses how auto-upgrade behavior changes to a 'slower cadence and higher stability'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response mentions Extended channel with '24-month support, extra cost only during extended period' as an option for maximum control and slower upgrade cycles

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: While response shows how to configure maintenance windows post-migration, it does not advise coordinating the actual channel change during a maintenance window
